data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "The collapse was a deliberate, low-tech cyber-attack analogue where rival powers introduced a memetic ideological virus rejecting divine kingship, causing mass refusal of labor tribute without physical conquest.", "mechanism": "Ideological contagion leading to non-violent systemic paralysis and administrative abandonment.", "scope": {"covers": ["Ugarit", "Mycenaean Greece", "Hittite Anatolia"], "excludes": ["Egypt", "Assyria"]}, "forbidden": [{"case": "Inscriptions or tablets from the transition period explicitly celebrating the restoration of traditional palace rituals by the same local populations immediately after destruction layers", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}], "prose_notes": "Highly atypical; posits that the 'destruction' layers represent ritual decommissioning or looting by locals after the system voluntarily dissolved, rather than external invasion or natural disaster."}
